title: Anchorage Daily News: Alaska's Newspaper
summary: Outdoors hot line Published: December 11, 2005 Last Modified: December 11, 2005 at 03:05 AM BOATING 'Water Wise' boating safety class set for this month The Alaska Office of Boating Safety offers the "Alaska Water Wise" boating course from 8 a.m. to 5 p.m. Saturday at the Atwood Building in downtown Anchorage. The class, approved by the National Association of State Boating Law Administrators, is free. Preregistration is encouraged. The course satisfies most states' boating safety education requirements and may qualify boaters for insurance discounts. For information or to register, contact Joe McCullough at 269-8704. title: Anchorage Daily News: Alaska's Newspaper
summary: Craig Medred: Alaska wilderness devours the deluded, ignorant and ill (8-21-2005) CRAIG MEDRED OUTDOORS Published: August 21, 2005 Last Modified: December 20, 2005 at 06:18 AM Wilderness shows no compassion for those who struggle with life, so we should take a moment to contemplate the sad fate of Richard Hasbell. The 34-year-old Anchorage man struggling with mental illness disappeared into Denali National Park and Preserve in July. An extensive and costly search for him largely ended this week. "Our thoughts are with the family, who don't yet have an answer to the question of what may have happened to Richard and where he is,'' Park Superintendent Paul Anderson said. Nobody knows for sure why Hasbell went into the wilderness. A brother has speculated that maybe "he had a breakdown and may…
